title: Festuca elwendiana
taxonomy: Festuca elwendiana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Festuca, which comprises various species of grasses commonly known as fescues. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots, including various types of grasses. Within the Poaceae family, Festuca elwendiana is likely to be classified as a type of cool-season grass. This classification is based on its growth habits and characteristics.

intro: Festuca elwendiana, also known as Elwend's fescue, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Festuca elwendiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Elwend's fescue include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to promote digestive health.
